problem.title: Course Schedule
problem.description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that you must take course bi first if you want to take course ai.
assumptions: prerequisites[i] = [a, b] means b must be taken before a.
Return true if you can finish all courses (no cycle exists in the prerequisite graph).

example.explanation: Take course 0 first, then course 1. No cycle → possible.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Topological Sort with DFS [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Model courses as a directed graph and detect cycles using DFS with a recursion-stack (grey/white/black coloring). If a DFS path revisits a node currently on the recursion stack, a cycle exists and the schedule is impossible. If no cycle is found across all nodes, all courses can be completed.

complexity.time: O(V + E) where V is vertices and E is edges

solutions.title: Solution 2 – BFS Topological Sort (Kahn's Algorithm) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Build an adjacency list and an in-degree array. Initialize a queue with all nodes whose in-degree is 0. Process: for each dequeued node, decrement the in-degree of all its neighbors; if a neighbor's in-degree drops to 0, enqueue it. Count nodes processed. If count == numCourses, no cycle exists and all courses can be finished. O(V+E) time O(V+E) space.
